race_name: L'Etape Brasil by Tour de France

top_categories.workout_context: 114ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Race-pace efforts that mimic L'Etape Brasil by Tour de France's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before September.
At L'Etape Brasil by Tour de France, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
7,546ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
7,546ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
L'Etape Brasil by Tour de France's paved mountain roads through the Serra da forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 66 miles.
66 miles on paved mountain roads through the Serra da burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of L'Etape Brasil by Tour de France. Train it until it feels boring.
Low-cadence force work for 7,546ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Base fitness for L'Etape Brasil by Tour de France. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 7,546ft of climbing on paved mountain roads through the Serra da does to your power file on race day.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved mountain roads through the Serra da. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for L'Etape Brasil by Tour de France’s 66 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at L'Etape Brasil by Tour de France. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on TT Threshold, Race Simulation, and Over Under to prepare you for 66 miles of paved mountain roads through the serra da mantiqueira in Campos do Jordao, Sao Paulo State, Brazil (flagship stage; series also visits Cunha and Rio de Janeiro).
